pattern: A mountain shape is fully characterized by a single peak reached through a strictly rising run and left through a strictly falling run, with both runs required to be non-empty.
hints: Walk forward from the start while each value is strictly greater than the one before it; note how far that climb reaches.
From that same point, walk forward while each value is strictly less than the one before it; the walk must reach the very end of the array.
Both the climb and the descent must actually move at least one step — a flat start or a flat ending disqualifies the array even if the rest looks right.

edge_inputs.description: too short to ever be a mountain
a plateau instead of a genuine peak
the shortest possible valid mountain, length three
the shortest possible length but strictly increasing only, no descent
strictly decreasing throughout, no rising part at all
a flat stretch sits right at the would-be peak
a flat stretch appears partway down the descent
peak and edges touch the extremes of the value range

stress.description: mix of genuine mountains and unconstrained random arrays
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n_cap = max(3, min(size, 10000))
    if rng.random() < 0.5:
        n = rng.randint(3, min(300, n_cap))
        peak = rng.randint(1, n - 2)
        vals = [0] * n
        v = rng.randint(0, 50)
        vals[0] = v
        for i in range(1, peak + 1):
            v = min(v + rng.randint(1, 3), 10000)
            vals[i] = v
        for i in range(peak + 1, n):
            v = max(v - rng.randint(1, 3), 0)
            vals[i] = v
        return [vals]
    else:
        n = rng.randint(1, n_cap)
        vals = [rng.randint(0, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
        return [vals]
